name: Captain Harl
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, battle-worn city captain on battlements, rain on armor, stern eyes, torchlight, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: No speeches. No prayers. Hold the wall.

nodes.text: If goblins slip past, they’ll set the city alight. Chase them if you must—just don’t lose the gate for it.

choices.label: Request a city whistle for chases.

name: Councilor Vayne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, stern city councilor in dark robes, candlelit council chamber, battle maps on table, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: You did not die. Excellent. Tell me what you changed.

nodes.text:
We measure victories in hours, not songs.

If you cut ladders, poisoned rations, burned supplies—we felt it.

If infiltrators slipped through, we feel that too.

name: Archivist Salenne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, mage archivist with crystal ball light, chalk runes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: The ball shows a single, clean truth: the next assault is their biggest.

nodes.text:
They will push three fronts:

1) A siege tower at the main wall.
2) A ram at the gate.
3) A drains surge—if the ‘second mouth’ still lives.

If you choose correctly, thousands live.

If not, the city becomes a corridor.

choices.label: Ask how to counter-signal under pressure.
Ask how to blunt the drains surge.

name: Guildmaster Orrow
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, stern guildmaster smith with leather apron, glowing forge behind, guild seals on wall, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: You fought like a veteran. Now you’ll be equipped like one—if you pay in sweat and sense.

nodes.text:
The guild will back you.

Bring components and I’ll grant you one of these:

- Warded leather reinforcements.
- A named blade fitting.
- A shield boss etched for enchantment.

Or a credit chit, if you’re broke and useful.

name: Prefect Luvara
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, scribe prefect in tall hat, ink-stained fingers, scroll racks, candle glow, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Ink is slower than steel, but it wins wars that steel cannot.

nodes.text:
We can issue you sanctioned spellwork—if you keep the city’s interests first.

Bring blank scrolls, rare inks, or proofs of enemy movements.

And mind this: reckless magic gets people hanged, not celebrated.

name: Councilor Vayne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, councilor in dawn light, city skyline smoking behind window, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: We survived. That’s not a victory. It’s permission to continue.

nodes.text:
You will be named Ironleaf Warden.

You will receive authority.

And you will receive enemies—inside the council and beyond the walls.

Now tell me: do you pursue the retreating host, or stay and rebuild?

choices.label: Choose pursuit as the next chapter.

name: Captain Harl
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, guard captain exhausted, soot on cheek, helmet under arm, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: We held the wall. The streets… depended on you more than you know.

nodes.text:
Reports:

- If arson damage is high: districts burned and panic lingers.
- If you stopped infiltrators: civilians are shaken but alive.

Either way, the enemy retreated.

Question is: do we let them regroup?

choices.label: Ask for a small squad escort (one use).

name: Quartermaster Brevin
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, tired quartermaster with ink-stained hands, stacks of crates, lantern light, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If it’s not nailed down, it’s already gone. If it is nailed down, someone is prying.

nodes.text: The council wants miracles. I settle for wagons that arrive and arrows that exist.

choices.label: Offer help moving supplies to the battlements.

name: Scribe Orrin
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, young scribe with spectacles and quills, rune-lit desk, scroll racks, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you brought blank scrolls, I can make them priceless. If you didn’t, please don’t bleed on my floor.

nodes.text: We’re binding ward-lines faster than we can copy them. Blank stock is life.

name: Clerk Elm
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, city clerk in plain robes, sharp eyes, wax seals and ledgers, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Speak clearly. The city has no patience left for confusion.

nodes.text: We issue writs, warrants, and consequences.

choices.label: Exchange 5 Bronze Sigils for 1 Silver Sigil.
Issue a Council Warrant (costs 2 Silver Sigils).

name: Harrow the Smith
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, blacksmith with soot and sparks, heavy apron, stern eyes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you can’t pay, you can sweat.

name: Moss the Apothecary
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, apothecary with herb-stained hands, shelves of bottles, watchful gaze, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Everything heals or harms — sometimes both.

name: Ren the Fletcher
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, fletcher artisan with feathers and wax, calm focus, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Keep your bow dry. Keep your hands steady.

name: Pella
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, streetwise ratcatcher with lantern and hooked pole, sewer backdrop, sharp grin, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Down there? It’s not ‘dark’. It’s hungry.

nodes.text:
I’ll guide you to the old underways.

But you pay in safety: don’t kick doors, don’t shout names, and if you smell mushrooms—hold your breath.

name: Archivist Salenne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, mage archivist with crystal ball glow on face, scrolls and candle smoke, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you brought the city its sight, then listen carefully—because sight is useless without action.

nodes.text:
The ball shows three threats:

1) Fire at the granary.
2) Breach attempt at the postern.
3) Knifers in the drains.

And one more thing: a city hand delivering wax-sealed orders to the enemy.

A ring. Black wax. A courier’s seal used from inside our walls.

name: Councilor Vayne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, councilor with battle map, ink stains, hard eyes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: This is the day the city becomes a story—or a warning.

nodes.text:
Your actions changed the board:

- If wheels were sabotaged: the siege tower arrives late or breaks.
- If pitch was ruined: fewer burning pours on the wall.
- If the ram chain was taken: the gate holds longer.
- If the second mouth is sealed: fewer knives inside.

Now choose where *you* stand.

name: Serjeant Mara
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, serjeant in rain and soot, spear tip nicked, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Pick a lane and commit. Heroes die when they try to be everywhere.

nodes.text:
Three lanes:

1) Wall line.
2) Gatehouse.
3) Streets (chase-down response and fires).

If you leave the streets unattended, the city burns from within even if the wall holds.

name: Lieutenant Kess
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, scout lieutenant grim, snowfall, torchlight on armor, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: We end this now, or we end it later with more graves.

nodes.text:
The Warlord’s survivors are rallying at the fortress-heart.

Expect:
- chokepoints,
- skirmish lines,
- and a ritual meant to turn desperation into power.

If you carry the war-map tablet, we’ll avoid the worst traps.

If you have the oracle’s advantage, we’ll see ambushes before they breathe.

If you destroyed it… we fight honest.

Ready?

name: Serjeant Mara
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, gritty serjeant with scarred face and spear, battlements behind, rain and smoke, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Pick a front. If you try to fight everywhere, you die everywhere.

nodes.text:
Three threats:

- Siege tower at the main wall.
- Ram at the gate.
- Drains surge (if the mouth still lives).

Tell me where you go first.

name: Gatewarden Holt
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, weathered gatewarden with keys and iron gauntlet, torchlight, grim but steady, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: The gate doesn’t care who you are. It cares what you can prove.

nodes.text: If something’s wrong, it’ll be wrong at the worst moment. That’s how gates work.

choices.label: Request access to the inner mechanisms (emergency).

name: Factor Merris
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, well-dressed dock factor with ledger, polite smile, cold eyes, rainy dock lanterns, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Supplies are complex. Rumours are simple. Which do you trade in?

nodes.text: The docks are chaos. Losses happen. Accusations happen too. Usually from fools.

choices.label: Present a Council Warrant and demand the ledger.
If he resists, arrest him (guards will be called).

name: Captain Roth
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, battle-worn city captain with rain cloak and scar, tense jaw, banners behind, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you’re here, you’re either brave, useful, or lost. Pick one fast.

nodes.text: We’re minutes from a bad day. Tell me what you’ve fixed.

choices.label: Report the contraband disruption and sabotage evidence.
Begin the battle sequence (if the city is battle-ready).

name: Lieutenant Kess
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, scout lieutenant on mountain ridge, cloak snapping in wind, distant fortress silhouette, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: This is where the war learns to breathe again. Don’t let it.

nodes.text:
Two ways to the fortress:

1) The Narrow Pass — faster, watched by archers.
2) The Goat Path — slower, safer if you can climb.

And there’s a rumor:
A waterfall that hides an old smuggler’s throat into the deep.

Pick your risk.

name: Jan the Porter
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, frightened porter with bundle of keys on belt, fortress kitchens behind, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: I haul their barrels. I don’t want their war.

nodes.text:
If you don’t kill me, I’ll tell you three things:

- The quartermaster keeps cold-iron keys.
- The cistern hatch leads to older stone.
- The waterfall cave is real… but it listens.

Will you spare me?

choices.label: Threaten him for the keyring (risk alarm).

name: Rill Dane
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, exhausted farmer in torn shirt with rope burns, dirt-streaked face, defiant eyes, torchlit cage, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Don’t talk loud. They like it when we beg.

nodes.text: They’re feeding the siege with our grain. And they’re moving a ‘prize’ through the old sinkholes—deeper than these woods. Red Knife Hold. That name keeps coming up.

name: Lieutenant Kess
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, scout lieutenant in wind, hills behind, tired eyes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: They’re retreating like wounded wolves. Still dangerous. Still clever.

nodes.text:
Trail signs:

- A runner carrying signals to an underground hold.
- Wargs scouting the flanks.
- A disciplined captain trying to keep order without the standard.

If we catch the runner, we choose the next battlefield.
If we fail, they vanish into stone.

choices.label: Commit to hunting the runner first.
Commit to breaking the captain’s rearguard first.

name: Marta of the Ridge Farms
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, farm woman in cloak, windblown hair, bruised but resolute, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: They took my boy to haul timber. Said the ‘stone city’ below would pay for him.

nodes.text:
I saw them vanish into a crack in the hills.

Not a cave.

A doorway shaped like old teeth.

If you go… bring a lantern. The dark down there is greedy.

name: Lady Seren Arclight
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, young noblewoman with dirt-streaked face, hair pinned back, chain marks on wrists, determined eyes, torchlit cavern, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you’re here to trade, don’t. If you’re here to fight, do it fast.

nodes.text: They aren’t holding me for ransom alone. They want the gate opened from within. There’s a ‘second mouth’ into the drains—mapped, measured, ready.

choices.label: Promise: I’ll get you out alive.

name: Mip
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, small goblin with torn hood, wide fearful eyes, holding a cracked token, cavern shadows, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Don’t kill me! I know where they keep the iron wedges. I know where the roof is weak.

nodes.text:
Captain Varg will trade. He loves trades. But he cheats. Always.

If you want to collapse the tunnel, the ‘breath pillars’ are the key. Three of them.

name: Master of Stores Rell
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, quartermaster in ledger room, keys at belt, wary expression, lantern light, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you need supplies, file a request like everyone else—oh, you don’t have time. No one does.

nodes.text:
Requisitions? Wax seals? I stamp what the council authorizes.

Unless… someone has a seal they shouldn’t.

name: Lieutenant Kess
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, scout lieutenant in mud-stained cloak, campfires behind, tense whisper, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: That camp runs on fear and a banner. Take the banner, and fear turns sideways.

nodes.text:
Their standard is kept below the command trench.

They call it a ‘vault’—it’s more like a buried shrine.

If you have the codebook, you can fake a horn pattern to pull guards away—once.

name: Lieutenant Kess
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, scout lieutenant muddy cloak, eyes reflecting campfire, tense, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: They rebuild confidence with wood and iron. Break the machines and you break their courage.

nodes.text:
Three targets. You can’t guarantee all three.

1) Siege tower wheels.
2) Gate ram head.
3) Pitch cauldrons.

If you kept the banner secret, they won’t be looking for you.
If you revealed it publicly, expect tighter patrols.

name: Elin of the Far Farms
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, exhausted carpenter prisoner behind bars, splinters in hands, determined eyes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you’re here to kill us, do it quick. If you’re here to help… listen.

nodes.text:
The wheelhouse hub has a soft join—hammer a wedge there and the tower limps.

The ram head chain is kept by the foreman.

And the pitch pits… sand ruins it better than water.

choices.label: Promise to free the cages if possible.

name: Liora Vale
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, noble courier woman with torn cloak, stubborn gaze, bruised but unbroken, torchlit dungeon, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you’ve come to bargain, I have nothing. If you’ve come to rescue, be quiet.

nodes.text: They took me for what I carried — not who I am. Their officers speak of a ‘sight orb’ being moved deeper. And they’re terrified of losing it.

name: Old Scribe
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, elderly scribe in rags, ink-stained fingers, haunted eyes, dungeon bars, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Walls have ears. Floors have teeth.

nodes.text: There’s a maintenance slit behind the cistern. Leads to the chain gallery. Don’t go where the drums echo.

choices.label: Thank him and ask about keys.

name: Tess of the Tower
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, young mage apprentice in torn robe, glowing runes on wrists, determined eyes, reliquary cell, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Don’t touch the runes. They bite.

nodes.text: The orb isn’t just seeing. It’s steering. It ‘nudges’ troop movement—like a hand on a chess piece. Break its cradle and it loses focus for days.

choices.label: Ask how to break the cradle.

name: Adept Sorn
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, nervous ward adept with inked fingers, cracked ward charm, guilty eyes, torchlight, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: You shouldn’t be here. Neither should I.

nodes.text: Give me a way out and I’ll mark the safe path through the ward lines. They’ll kill me when the orb is moved.

choices.label: Accept: mark me a safe route.

name: The Bound Oracle
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, chained oracle figure fused with crystal and stone, pale glow, sorrowful eyes, runes on chains, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: I am the map they bleed through.

nodes.text:
They chained me to predict.

To choose.

To win.

If you free me, I may flee… or I may repay.

If you bind me for the city, you make the city dangerous.

If you destroy me, you end the advantage—forever.

Choose with clean hands, if you can.

choices.label: Ask how to break the Heartspike safely.
Ask how to rebind it for the city without cruelty.

name: Councilor Vayne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, tired councilor with sharp eyes, candlelit war room, maps and seals, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: You brought back the war’s hidden organs. Now the city must decide how to use them.

nodes.text:
We have three crises:

1) The Warlord is rallying survivors.
2) Someone inside the city profited from the siege.
3) The streets still whisper panic.

You may choose only two actions before dawn.

Choose like a veteran.

Choose like a knife.

choices.label: Prioritise calming the city (morale, riots, fires).

name: Councilor Mirelda
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, stern councilor woman in civic robes, city watch banners, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: Victory is not a feast. It is a duty. And duty requires order.

nodes.text:
Give me a safe-conduct decree and I can lock the city down without cruelty.

Refuse, and the watch will do it anyway—just with panic and clubs.

What do you want the streets to remember?

name: Master Warden Sell
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, thin master warden with iron chain insignia, shadowed archive behind, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: There is always a second battle after the siege: the battle over truth.

nodes.text:
I can trace sabotage… if you can bring me proof.

A ledger. A seal. A confession.

But be warned:
Some truths kill more cleanly than swords.

If you proceed, you may end up with enemies in silk.

choices.label: Keep focus on the Warlord; ignore internal rot for now.

name: Guildmaster Orrow
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, guildmaster at forge holding gleaming blade fitting, sparks, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you’re going after the Warlord, you don’t go with beginner steel.

nodes.text:
I can upgrade ONE named weapon into an heirloom.

Bring:
- a Sunsteel Blade Fitting,
- and proof you’ll use it for the city.

Which weapon do you offer?

name: Sister Elin
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, weary healer in simple robes with bloodstained sleeves, infirmary lanterns, compassionate eyes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you can stand, you can carry water or bandages.

nodes.text:
We’re keeping people alive by inches.

If arson spreads, smoke kills faster than steel.

If knives are loose in the streets, the wounded never reach us.

name: Inquisitor Dran
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, hooded inquisitor with ink-stained gloves, small cell doorway, cold lantern light, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: They lie. You decide how quickly they stop.

nodes.text:
We caught a token carrier. Not a fighter—an errand goblin.

Offer mercy, offer fear, offer cleverness. Choose your method.

name: Lieutenant Kess
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, city scout lieutenant in hooded cloak, rain beading on leather, watchfort silhouette behind, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: We don’t have an army for this. We have you.

nodes.text:
That watchfort used to be ours. Now it’s a relay hub.

Steal their signal book, free our people, and cut the banner captain out of the chain.

If you’re seen, the siege line will learn the postern gate exists.

name: Sergeant Bram
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, bruised sergeant in torn tabard behind iron bars, stubborn eyes, torchlit pen, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: If you’re real, cut these bars. If you’re not, stop wasting my breath.

nodes.text:
The banner captain lives in the relay tower.

There’s a hidden sally hatch behind the armory rack—old city workmanship.

And there’s a troll on a chain. Don’t fight it in open ground.
